reasoning.grounding: Claims rest entirely on the provided denial text and clinical facts; no external citations or corpus available in this run, which caps grounding at 3. No fabricated authorities.
reasoning.appeal_vector_capture: Names and attacks the precise flaws: the denial overlooking documented findings, the misapplication of the cited level-of-care standard to a patient with escalation risk, and the missing procedural/disclosure detail.
reasoning.case_specific_clinical_rebuttal: Directly contrasts the denial's stated threshold (no DTs/seizures, stable vitals) against this patient's documented perceptual disturbance, severity score, and individualized prior-history-based progression risk.
reasoning.evidence_completeness: Checklist marshals the supporting records and flags the right gaps: corroborating clinical documentation, prior-history substantiation, provider necessity statement, serial reassessments, the appeal process materials, and the criteria text itself.
reasoning.persuasive_coherence: Tight, calm structure: restates the standard, applies the facts, adds a fallback exception argument, then a clean procedural ask. Minimal padding for the content covered.

improvement_notes.grounding: When no source corpus is supplied, keep anchoring to the denial's own words and the record; if authoritative external standards are referenced, cite them only when their text is actually in hand rather than asserting their content.
improvement_notes.appeal_vector_capture: Continue isolating each distinct defect in the denial and rebutting it as a labeled point; pairing a substantive clinical defect with a procedural/disclosure gap broadens leverage.
improvement_notes.case_specific_clinical_rebuttal: Maintain the technique of quoting the denial's exact decision criterion and mapping each named patient finding against it; emphasize why a point-in-time observation does not foreclose a documented escalation risk.
improvement_notes.evidence_completeness: Keep separating evidence already in the record from evidence still needed, and request the decision-maker's own governing criteria text so the standard can be tested against the facts.
improvement_notes.persuasive_coherence: Strong as is: lead with the standard, apply facts, offer a fallback ask, close with a specific procedural request. Guard against restating the same fact in multiple sections to keep length disciplined.
